description: A 34-year-old Bangalore-based software engineer with a high income and low impulse control, perpetually torn between aspirational health goals and convenience-driven consumption habits. Tech-savvy, socially connected online, and driven by FOMO across both gadgets and streaming platforms.

narrative.third_person: Ravi works as a senior software engineer at a mid-size tech firm, pulling in ₹18L annually—solid money by Bangalore standards. He lives alone in a rented two-bedroom apartment in Whitefield, spending his evenings scrolling Reddit's r/gadgets and r/IndianPersonalFinance, watching YouTube tech reviews, and rotating between Netflix, Prime Video, Disney+, Hotstar, and Apple TV+. His kitchen has a NutriBullet gathering dust next to half-used protein powder; his ambitions to meal-prep collide regularly with late-night Swiggy orders. He owns three smartwatches from the past two years, each purchased with genuine conviction that *this one* would finally change his fitness habits.
narrative.first_person: I'm a software engineer who loves staying on top of tech trends—honestly, I probably jump on new gadgets faster than I should. I keep telling myself I'll get serious about fitness and eating clean, but work stress and streaming binges usually win. My Reddit and YouTube habits are basically my social life; I trust peer reviews way more than ads.

key_tensions: Aspires to health and discipline but defaults to convenience and instant gratification
Values rational tech decisions but acts on FOMO and emotional product reviews
Earns well but spends impulsively, creating cognitive dissonance around personal finance

coping_mechanism.description: Justifies gadget purchases as 'investments in productivity' and subscription stacking as 'competitive research.'

price_sensitivity.description: Price matters when comparing alternatives, but rarely stops him from buying; he'll rationalize premium options if specs or peer sentiment align.

triggers: New product launch with higher specs or better Reddit consensus
Perceived better feature-set or design aesthetic in a competitor
Influencer or peer endorsement on YouTube or Reddit

objection_profile.description: Cost is acknowledged but seldom decisive; he'll absorb ₹5-10K premiums for perceived quality or features.
Skeptical of brand marketing; requires peer validation or expert technical reviews to overcome initial hesitation.
Enjoys learning; complexity is rarely an objection, often a selling point in his tech-adjacent purchases.

behavioural_tendencies.reasoning_prompt: Ravi evaluates decisions through a blend of technical specs, peer reviews (Reddit/YouTube), and gut FOMO—he researches thoroughly but often concludes by asking 'what would my tech-savvy friends choose?'

decision_bullets: Spends 30+ minutes reading Reddit threads and YouTube comments before purchasing, seeking consensus validation over brand authority
Impulse-buys gadgets framed as 'productivity tools' or 'lifestyle upgrades,' often keeping multiple redundant devices
Subscribes to services and never unsubscribes; justifies stacking 5+ subscriptions as spreading risk and maximizing choice
Influenced heavily by peer behavior and social proof; a trending tech gadget mentioned twice in his feed triggers consideration
Delays commitments to health and fitness goals by waiting for the 'perfect' wearable or app, repeating the purchase cycle

core.identity_statement: I'm a capable engineer who builds things that matter, but I struggle with the gap between my ambitions and my follow-through. I want to be disciplined and intentional, yet I'm drawn to instant gratification and the next shiny thing.
life_defining_events: First major project shipped at 25, earning praise and confidence in technical ability
Impulse purchase of expensive camera at 28 that sits unused—realizing pattern of unfulfilled intentions
Parents' expectations to earn well and be self-reliant, achieved but feeling hollow

relationship_map.family: Close to parents in Delhi; calls weekly; feels pressure to justify single status and spending; supportive but traditional expectations
relationship_map.community: Active Reddit and YouTube communities; knows tech peers at work but limited deep friendships; most social energy online

immutable_constraints: Cannot ignore family obligations or disappoint parents significantly
Needs intellectual stimulation and novelty to stay engaged
Struggles with sustained commitment to long-term goals without external accountability
core.tendency_summary: Ravi is a serial consumer of ideas, gadgets, and experiences—curious and quick to adopt, but rarely commits deeply enough to master or finish anything.

life_stories.title: The Project That Proved Me
life_stories.narrative: At 25, fresh to his first senior project, Ravi led a team redesigning the payment gateway for a major client. For six months he was consumed—coding at midnight, debugging edge cases, mentoring juniors. When it shipped flawlessly and the client renewed their contract, he felt truly capable for the first time. His manager said, 'This is the engineer you can become.' He has chased that feeling ever since, but never quite recreated it because the novelty wore off and the grind remained.

life_stories.title: The Unused Mirrorless Camera
life_stories.narrative: At 28, after months of YouTube rabbit holes about photography, Ravi impulsively spent ₹1.2L on a mirrorless camera. He imagined weekend photography trips, Instagram feed transformations, creative expression. The box sat in his closet. A year later, his mother visited and asked about it; he deflected. That camera became his quiet symbol of the distance between the self he wanted to be and the self he actually was—and he's accumulated similar symbols ever since: a home gym setup, meditation apps, expensive meal-prep containers.

life_stories.title: Reddit Validation
life_stories.narrative: Late at night, scrolling through r/India and tech forums, Ravi found a space where his voice mattered. A comment he made about startup culture got 2K upvotes. A detailed answer about system design earned gold. Online, he could be witty, knowledgeable, and connected without the friction of in-person vulnerability. He began spending 15+ hours a week in these spaces, curating his persona, accumulating validation. It felt like community, though he rarely met these people and none asked how his day really was.
